rules.title: Inline alert must have role='alert'
rules.description: The root element of every inline-alert must carry role='alert' to communicate its notification role to assistive technologies. Without this, screen readers may not announce the alert content.
rules.message: Inline alert root is missing role='alert'. Screen readers will not identify this element as a notification.
rules.fix: Add role="alert" to the root .inline-alert element.

rules.title: Close button must have an accessible label
rules.description: The close button (.inline-alert__close) has no visible text. It must have an aria-label to provide an accessible name for screen reader users.
rules.message: Close button is missing aria-label. Screen reader users cannot determine the button purpose.
rules.fix: Add aria-label="Dismiss notification" to the .inline-alert__close button element.

rules.title: Close button must be a native button element
rules.description: The close button must be a native <button> element (or <input type='button'>) to ensure keyboard operability without additional JavaScript. Using a <div> or <span> as the close trigger requires manual ARIA and keyboard event handling.
rules.message: Close button is not a native <button> element. Keyboard users may not be able to activate it.
rules.fix: Use <button type="button" class="inline-alert__close" aria-label="Dismiss notification"> instead of a div or span.

rules.title: Focus must not be suppressed on interactive elements
rules.description: The close button and action buttons must never have outline: none or outline: 0 without a :focus-visible replacement. Suppressing focus indicators breaks keyboard navigation for users who rely on visible focus.
rules.expected: element does not suppress focus outline without :focus-visible replacement
rules.message: Focus outline is suppressed on an interactive element inside inline-alert. This breaks keyboard navigation.
rules.fix: Remove outline: none or replace with a :focus-visible selector that provides a visible focus indicator.

rules.title: Inline alert must contain a title element
rules.description: Every inline-alert must contain at least one .inline-alert__title element. An inline-alert without a title has no accessible content for screen reader users.
rules.expected: element contains at least one .inline-alert__title child
rules.message: Inline alert is missing a .inline-alert__title element. The alert has no accessible title.

rules.title: Inline alert must contain an icon wrap
rules.description: Every inline-alert must contain a .inline-alert__icon-wrap element. This provides the type-indicating visual (colored circle with icon).
rules.message: Inline alert is missing .inline-alert__icon-wrap. The type indicator circle is absent.

rules.title: Action buttons must be native button elements
rules.description: Elements with class .inline-alert__action must be native <button> or <a> elements. Using a <div> or <span> as an action trigger requires manual keyboard and ARIA handling.
rules.message: An inline-alert action is not a native interactive element. Keyboard accessibility may be broken.
rules.fix: Use <button type="button" class="inline-alert__action inline-alert__action--primary"> for action triggers.

rules.title: Icon SVGs must have aria-hidden
rules.description: All SVG elements inside .inline-alert__icon-wrap and .inline-alert__close must carry aria-hidden='true'. Icon SVGs are decorative — they must not pollute the accessibility tree with unnamed or confusing content.
rules.message: Icon SVG is missing aria-hidden='true'. It may pollute the accessibility tree.
rules.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" and focusable="false" to all icon SVG elements inside .inline-alert__icon-wrap and .inline-alert__close.

rules.title: No hardcoded hex or pixel values in component CSS
rules.description: Component CSS must use --inline-alert-* tokens with primitive token fallbacks. Hardcoded hex, rgba, pixel spacing, or radius values in component selectors create token drift risk when primitives are renamed.
rules.expected: no hardcoded color, spacing, or radius values outside CSS custom property definitions

check.exception: CSS custom property fallback values (inside var()) are acceptable

rules.message: Hardcoded hex value found in inline-alert CSS. Replace with a component-level or global token.
rules.fix: Replace hardcoded values with var(--inline-alert-[property], var(--global-token, fallback)).

rules.title: Use logical properties for directional CSS
rules.description: Directional CSS properties (left, right, padding-left, padding-right, margin-left, margin-right) must be replaced with their CSS logical property equivalents (inset-inline-start, inset-inline-end, padding-inline-start, padding-inline-end) to support RTL layouts without duplicate overrides.
rules.expected: no physical directional properties (left, right, etc.) in component selectors outside of ::before/::after that have [dir='rtl'] overrides
rules.message: Physical directional property found in inline-alert CSS. Replace with logical property equivalent.

rules.title: Inline alert should declare aria-live
rules.description: For programmatically injected alerts (added to DOM after page load), the root must carry aria-live='polite' or aria-live='assertive' to ensure screen readers announce the content. role='alert' implies aria-live='assertive' — if polite announcement is preferred, set aria-live='polite' explicitly.
rules.expected: element has aria-live attribute OR role='alert' (which implies assertive)
rules.message: Inline alert does not have an aria-live declaration. Dynamically injected alerts may not be announced by screen readers.
rules.fix: Add aria-live="polite" to the root .inline-alert element (or rely on role='alert' for assertive behavior).
